markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Persist Monaco code block content, language, display options, diagnostics, and source links through rich document APIs. This MT belongs to TiptapMonacoIntegration. Integrate Tiptap and Monaco as the full-featured Handshake editor surface.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Tiptap/ProseMirror is the canonical rich document engine for this WP; Monaco is the canonical code-block/code editing surface embedded inside Handshake.
Tiptap and Monaco must be fully integrated into Handshake packaging, UI state, backend save/load, visual debugging, and tests; no separate external editor app may be required.
scope.expected_behavior: CodeBlockPersistenceBridge leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CodeBlockPersistenceBridge is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified d7e2b397: code_block_serialization.test.ts 5/5 - code-block persistence bridge (language+text+hash stable). TiptapMonacoIntegration group complete: full app vitest 60 files/442 tests green; tsc --noEmit + eslint clean; offline Playwright roundtrip PASS (bundled Monaco mounts + round-trips, ZERO external network requests, worker chunks loopback-only). RichTextEditor wired into RichDocumentView over the real rich-doc backend; no src/backend or .GOV product code touched.

findings.title: verifyCodeBlockIntegrity is never called by any product load path - the integrity check is dead in production
findings.evidence: grep across app/src: verifyCodeBlockIntegrity/deserializeCodeNode/serializeCodeNode appear ONLY in test files. code_block_serialization.ts:69-74 documents "compared on load to detect silent corruption ... surfaced by the load path (MT-174)", but RichDocumentView load (RichDocumentView.tsx:58-87) never verifies code-block hashes. Silent out-of-band corruption is NOT detected, contradicting the MT-168 stated purpose.
findings.fix: On document load, walk content_json monacoCodeBlock nodes and verifyCodeBlockIntegrity; surface a typed MT-174 error (kind index or a new integrity kind) listing the failing blocks; add the load-path test.

findings.title: Upstream attr writes can mint hash/language mismatches (cross-ref MT-165 stale-closure bug)
findings.evidence: The single-minting-point claim (makeCodeBlockAttrs, code_block_serialization.ts:52-65) is bypassed by MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx:73 which computes codeBlockHash(staleLanguage, next) directly. The bridge invariant (hash always derived from current language+code) is violated by its main writer.
findings.fix: Route ALL attr writes through makeCodeBlockAttrs(currentLanguage, code) (no direct codeBlockHash calls in views); fix the stale closure per MT-165 review.

findings.title: Hash input concatenation has a theoretical ambiguity
findings.evidence: codeBlockHash uses `${language} ${code}`; a language id containing a space would alias pairs. Registry ids contain no spaces today, so this is latent only.
findings.fix: Use a non-collidable separator (e.g. language.length prefix or \u0000) or document the no-space invariant where ids are minted.

parity_gaps: Round-trip integrity: persisted but unverified on load -> the §7.1.1.8 deterministic round-trip assertion is not actually enforced at runtime
missing_tests: document load with a tampered code-block hash surfaces a typed integrity error (blocked: not wired)
NodeView edit after language switch keeps hash valid (cross-ref MT-165)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
